title: Preserve every callee-saved register you use
description: Save and restore the ABI's callee-saved registers in the prologue/epilogue so the caller's values survive the call.
body: Use when a function uses more registers than the scratch (caller-saved) set. Do not write to a callee-saved register (e.g. rbx/rbp/r12-r15 on x86-64, x19-x28 on AArch64) without saving it first, and do not forget to restore it on every return path. Actions: in the prologue push/store each callee-saved register you will clobber, restore them in the epilogue (including early returns), and keep the save/restore balanced. Validation signals include the disassembly showing matched save/restore on all paths and the caller's register values intact after the call. Risk boundary: clobbering a callee-saved register corrupts the caller silently and the failure appears unrelated; treat an unbalanced or missing restore as a blocking defect.
